# Coding Agent Feature Matrix
> Compare 24 coding agents, CLIs, and IDEs across features.
> https://compare.ainorthstar.tech | Updated 2026-06-11

Agent             Rel    FF     Fre   NoA   Oth   Rul   Skl   Hks   MCP   Cmd   Gol   Sub   Trn   Mod   App   San   Res   Con   Hdl   Fmt   Sta   Tel   Hst  BYOM
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Aider             Jun '2 CLI        Y     Y     Y     ~     ~     ~     N     N     N     ~     ~     Y     ~     N     N     ~     Y     N     N     Y     N     Y
Amp               May '2 CLI/Ext    Y                 Y     Y     Y     Y     N     N     Y     ?     ~     ~     N     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     ?     N     ~
Antigravity       2025  CLI/IDE    Y                 Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     N     ?     ~     ?     ~     ~     Y     Y     Y     ?     ?     ?     N     ?
Claude Code       Feb '2 CLI/Ext                      Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     ~
Cline             Jul '2 CLI/Ext    Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     ?     Y     Y     ~     Y     ~     Y     Y     N     Y     N     Y
OpenAI Codex CLI  Apr '2 CLI/Ext    Y           Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y
Cohere North      Jan '2 SDK/Web                      ~     ~     Y     Y     N     N     Y     ~     ~     ~     Y     ~     ~     ~     ~     N     ~     Y     N
Cursor            2023  CLI/IDE    Y                 Y     Y     ?     Y     Y     N     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     ~     Y     Y     Y
Devin             Dec '2 CLI/Ext    Y                 Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     ~     ?     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     N     ?     Y     ?
Factory Droid     2025  CLI/Ext          ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ?     ~     Y
Gemini CLI        Jun '2 CLI        Y           Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     N     ?     ?     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     ?     Y     N     ~
GitHub Copilot    Jun '2 CLI/IDE                      Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     N     Y     ?     Y     ~     ~     Y     Y     Y     ~     N     ~     Y     Y
Grok Build        May '2 CLI                          Y     Y     Y     Y     ?     N     Y     ?     ~     Y     ~     ?     ?     Y     ?     ?     ?     N     ~
Google Jules      Aug '2 Web                          ?     N     ~     ?     N     N     ~     ~     ~     Y     Y     ~     ~     ~     ~     N     ?     Y     N
JetBrains Junie   Jan '2 CLI/IDE                      ~     ?     N     ?     ?     N     ?     ?     ~     Y     ~     ~     ~     ?     ?     N     ?     N     ~
Kilo Code         Feb '2 CLI/Ext    Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     ?     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     ~     Y
Kimi CLI          Oct '2 CLI/Ext                      Y     Y     ~     Y     ~     N     Y     ?     Y     Y     N     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ?     N     Y
Kiro              Jul '2 CLI/IDE    Y                 Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     ~     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     ~     ?     ?     ~     N
OpenCode          2025  CLI/Ext    Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     Y     Y     ?     Y     N     Y
Pi                May '2 CLI/SDK          Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     N     N     Y     Y     N     N     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     ~     N     Y
Qwen Code         Jun '2 CLI/Ext                      Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     ?     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     ~     Y
Replit Agent      Sep '2 Web                          ~     N     ~     ?     N     N     ~     ~     N     ~     Y     ~     ~     N     N     N     ?     Y     N
Roo Code          Mid-20 Extensi                      Y     Y     N     Y     Y     N     Y     ?     Y     Y     N     ~     Y     Y     ~     N     Y     N     Y
Windsurf Cascade  Nov '2 CLI/IDE                      Y     Y     Y     Y     Y     N     Y     ?     Y     Y     ~     Y     Y     ~     ~     ~     ~     Y     ~

## Feature legend
Code = Y: full | ~: partial | N: none | ?: unknown |  : 
FF = Form factor (CLI/IDE/Ext/SDK/Web)

## Agent details

### Aider
- Docs: https://aider.chat/docs/
- GitHub: https://github.com/Aider-AI/aider
- Website: https://aider.chat/
- Form factor: CLI
- Released: Jun '23
- Latest update: May '26
- Some Free Usage: full — Aider documents free API providers and local-model options, so some agent usage can be free depending on provider/model choice.
- No Account Required: full — Can run against local Ollama models without signing into an Aider-hosted account.
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: full — Can use external provider accounts/API keys and local OpenAI-compatible endpoints instead of an Aider-owned subscription.
- Rules: partial — Supports CONVENTIONS.md loaded via --read or .aider.conf.yml. No equivalent of a persistent auto-loaded CLAUDE.md file. Location: `.aider.conf.yml`, `.aider.model.settings.yml`, `.aider.model.metadata.json`, and repo convention files; no AGENTS.md standard.
- Skills: partial — Has /save and /load commands to persist/replay sequences of slash commands. Not a full reusable skill system like Cursor skills. Location: no first-class skill folder; use repo files/prompts/conventions.
- Hooks: partial — Only git pre-commit hook bypass via --git-commit-verify. No lifecycle hooks (session-start, session-end, etc.).
- Mcp Servers: none — No MCP (Model Context Protocol) support detected in documentation, codebase, or feature listings. Location: no MCP config for Aider.
- Custom Commands: none — 40+ built-in slash commands but no mechanism for users to define custom slash commands. Location: no custom command folder; uses CLI flags/config.
- Goal Command: none — In-chat `/` commands (e.g. `/add`, `/commit`) are built-in chat commands, not a persisted `/goal` loop with pause/resume until a completion condition.
- Subagents: partial — Architect/Editor mode uses two models but no general subagent delegation system. No spawning of independent worker agents.
- Transcripts: partial — Location: optional chat history/transcript files are configured with Aider history settings such as `.aider.chat.history.md`.
- Model Selection: full — Full model selection via --model, --weak-model, --editor-model. In-chat /model command. Model aliases supported.
- Approval Mode: partial — Has --yes-always to auto-accept all confirmations. No granular approval mode like Cursor's always/accept-tool/plan-then-act.
- Sandbox Mode: none — No filesystem or network sandbox. Has --dry-run flag that prevents file modification. Aider edits files directly in the working git repo.
- Resume: none — No --resume flag. Has --restore-chat-history which restores previous chat messages but does not resume session state.
- Continue: partial — No --continue flag for auto-resuming the latest session. --restore-chat-history restores message history but is manual.
- Non Interactive: full — Full non-interactive mode via --message/-m (send instruction, edit files, exit). Also --apply, --commit, --lint, --test.
- Output Format: none — No structured machine-readable output format (JSON, etc.). Has --no-pretty for plain text, --verbose for debug info.
- Statusline: none — No configurable status line. Has extensive color customization but no status bar/line concept.
- Telemetry: full — Opt-in anonymous analytics via PostHog. Permanently disable with --analytics-disable. Never collects code/chat/keys.
- Hosted Agent: none — Runs entirely locally via pip. No hosted agent platform.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Connects to any LLM via litellm. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama, OpenRouter, any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, and more.
- Pricing: Free (Apache-2.0)
- Notes: Aider is a mature open-source CLI coding agent (45k+ stars) since June 2023. Deep git integration, repo mapping for codebase context, extensive LLM provider support through litellm. No published embed SDK (CLI/scripting only). Notable gaps vs newer agents: no MCP, no user-defined slash commands, no sandbox, no subagent delegation, no resume/continue flags, no structured output. Apache-2.0 licensed.

### Amp
- Docs: https://ampcode.com/manual
- Website: https://ampcode.app/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK
- Released: May '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Some Free Usage: full — Amp documents a free mode for interactive editor/CLI use, with limits and availability caveats.
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: full — Full support via AGENTS.md files (plural). Scanned from cwd, parent dirs, subtrees, $HOME/.config/amp/, /etc/ampcode/. Supports @-mentions, globs, YAML frontmatter. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Skills: full — Full support. Skills are directories with SKILL.md + YAML frontmatter. Locations: .agents/skills/ (project), ~/.config/agents/skills/ (user). Can bundle MCP servers via mcp.json. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Hooks: full — Full support via Plugin API (TypeScript). Events: session.start, agent.start, tool.call, tool.result, agent.end. Plugins can respond with allow/reject/modify/synthesize actions.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full support. Add via CLI (amp mcp add) or config. Local (command/args/env) and remote (url/headers) servers. OAuth auto-flow. Skill bundling for lazy loading. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Custom Commands: none — Removed January 29, 2026. Replaced by skills and command palette (Ctrl+O) instead of slash commands. Location: not applicable; feature not supported.
- Goal Command: none — Amp documents CLI and editor workflows but no built-in `/goal` slash command for persistent session objectives.
- Subagents: full — Full support. Spawned via Task tool. Built-in specialized subagents: Oracle (GPT-5.4), Librarian (code search), Painter (image gen), Review, Search.
- Transcripts: unknown — Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.
- Model Selection: partial — Pre-set modes (smart=Claude Opus 4.7, deep=GPT-5.5 reasoning, rush=GPT-5.5 no-reasoning, free=mixed). You choose the mode, not the exact model.
- Approval Mode: partial — Default: no approval (dangerously-allow-all is now default). Custom approval policies possible via Plugin API (tool.call event).
- Sandbox Mode: none — No built-in sandbox. Documentation suggests external isolated dev environments for untrusted code.
- Resume: full — Full support. 'amp threads continue [thread id]' documented. Thread referencing (@T-xxx or URL) across sessions.
- Continue: full — Full support. /continue command in CLI to pick old threads. Threads persist at ampcode.com/threads.
- Non Interactive: full — Full support. amp --execute / amp -x for headless mode. Set AMP_API_KEY env var for CI/CD. Auto-detects piped stdout.
- Output Format: full — Full support. --stream-json flag with --execute for Claude Code-compatible NDJSON streaming output. Multiple schema types.
- Statusline: none — Not supported. Custom themes removed in the May 6, 2026 rebuild. No statusline customization.
- Telemetry: unknown — No explicit telemetry settings found in documentation. No on/off telemetry toggle mentioned in manual or settings.
- Hosted Agent: none — CLI runs locally. Threads shared via web but no managed agent platform.
- Custom Model Provider: partial — No BYOM for the main agent (picks models per mode from providers). MCP servers can connect to external tools, and Plugin API allows custom tool registration.
- Pricing: Usage-based with free tier
- Notes: Amp is opinionated and aggressively removes features it considers obsolete (custom commands, slash commands, themes). Focuses on frontier models. Plugin API provides extensive extensibility for those who need it. Closed-source product; some auxiliary tools open source on GitHub.

### Antigravity
- Docs: https://antigravity.google/docs/
- Website: https://antigravity.google/
- Form factor: CLI, IDE
- Released: 2025
- Latest update: May '26
- Some Free Usage: full — Google announced Antigravity public preview availability at no charge with rate limits.
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: full — Full plugin rules on CLI; IDE has persistent agent context with overlapping rules support. Location: Antigravity plugin rules live in plugin folders; workspace/global IDE rule storage is managed by Antigravity.
- Skills: full — Plugin skills on CLI; IDE local data includes skills with less public doc detail. Location: plugin skill folders containing `SKILL.md`.
- Hooks: full — Plugin hooks documented on CLI.
- Mcp Servers: full — CLI plugin mcp_config.json; IDE also ships MCP configuration. Location: plugin `mcp_config.json` files and IDE/CLI MCP configuration.
- Custom Commands: partial — CLI via plugin capabilities; IDE via global workflows rather than classic slash-command folders. Location: command-like behavior is via plugins/workflows rather than a documented standalone command folder.
- Goal Command: none — Antigravity CLI docs cover plugins, sandbox, and slash commands but do not document a Codex-style `/goal` primitive.
- Subagents: unknown
- Transcripts: partial — Location: Antigravity brain/trajectory summaries are under `~/.gemini/antigravity/brain/<uuid>/*.md`; index data is in `~/Library/Application Support/Antigravity/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb`.
- Model Selection: unknown — IDE model UI exists; agy --help does not expose a model flag as of this dataset.
- Approval Mode: partial — CLI supports --dangerously-skip-permissions; IDE approval behavior not fully documented.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — CLI supports --sandbox for terminal restrictions.
- Resume: full — CLI --continue/--conversation; IDE stores conversation/brain state.
- Continue: full — CLI --continue for latest conversation; IDE continues from existing history.
- Non Interactive: full — CLI supports --print / --prompt; IDE is interactive-first.
- Output Format: unknown
- Statusline: unknown
- Telemetry: unknown
- Hosted Agent: none — Local IDE and CLI surfaces.
- Custom Model Provider: unknown — No arbitrary provider configuration found in public CLI help/docs.
- Pricing: Free (generous weekly rate limits), Google AI Pro/Ultra (undisclosed), Organization (consumption-based via Google Cloud)
- Notes: Google Antigravity spans an agentic IDE and the agy CLI. Distinct from deprecated Gemini CLI. No published programmatic SDK.

### Claude Code
- Docs: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview
- GitHub: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code
- Website: https://www.anthropic.com/claude-code
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK, Web, Mac App
- Released: Feb '25
- Latest update: Jun '26
- Some Free Usage: blank
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: full — Uses CLAUDE.md memory files. Location: `CLAUDE.md` in repo tree, plus `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` for user memory.
- Skills: full — Supports skills. Location: `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` or user-level `~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`.
- Hooks: full — Supports lifecycle hooks.
- Mcp Servers: full — Supports MCP servers. Location: project `.mcp.json` or Claude Code MCP config via `claude mcp`.
- Custom Commands: full — Supports slash commands. Location: `.claude/commands/*.md` or `~/.claude/commands/*.md`.
- Goal Command: full — Built-in `/goal` sets a completion condition; after each turn a fast evaluator decides whether Claude should continue until the condition holds (Claude Code v2.1.139+).
- Subagents: full — Supports custom subagents.
- Transcripts: full — Location: Claude Code CLI transcripts are JSONL under `~/.claude/projects/<proj-path-dashes>/<sessionId>.jsonl`.
- Model Selection: full — Model can be configured.
- Approval Mode: full — Permission mode is configurable.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Permissions and allowed tools provide sandbox-like controls.
- Resume: full — Supports --resume.
- Continue: full — Supports --continue for the most recent conversation.
- Non Interactive: full — Supports --print.
- Output Format: full — Supports output format controls.
- Statusline: full — Supports custom status line.
- Telemetry: full — Settings include telemetry and privacy controls.
- Hosted Agent: partial — CLI runs locally; managed/cloud-based session execution is not directly exposed as a distinct platform.
- Custom Model Provider: partial — Supports Bedrock/Vertex and related integrations, not arbitrary providers in the same way as OpenAI-compatible CLIs.
- Pricing: Included in Pro $20/mo, Max 5x $100/mo, Max 20x $200/mo, Team $25-125/seat/mo, Enterprise $20/seat + API rates

### Cline
- Docs: https://docs.cline.bot/
- GitHub: https://github.com/cline/cline
- Website: https://cline.bot/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK
- Released: Jul '24
- Latest update: Jun '26
- Some Free Usage: full — Cline is free for individual developers; AI inference is paid separately or BYOK/local.
- No Account Required: full — Can run with local models via Ollama or LM Studio without a Cline-hosted account.
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: full — Cline documents OpenAI Codex OAuth so users can bring a ChatGPT subscription, and also supports BYOK providers.
- Rules: full — Rich rule system: .clinerules/ directory, global ~/Documents/Cline/Rules/, conditional rules with YAML frontmatter globs, auto-detection of .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, AGENTS.md. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Skills: full — Full skill system: SKILL.md files in .cline/skills/ or ~/.cline/skills/, YAML frontmatter, progressive loading, slash command triggering, skill toggles. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Hooks: full — Lifecycle hooks via SDK plugin system: 18 hook stages (input, runtime_event, session_start, turn_start, tool_call_before/after, etc.). Also ~/.cline/hooks/ directory.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full MCP support with STDIO and HTTP/SSE transports, MCP Marketplace for one-click install, cline mcp CLI wizard. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Custom Commands: full — Built-in slash commands plus skills auto-register as slash commands. SDK plugins can also register custom commands. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Goal Command: none — Cline supports custom slash commands via workflow files; no built-in `/goal` for auto-continuing until a completion condition.
- Subagents: full — Full subagent system: use_subagents tool spawns parallel read-only research agents with separate context/token budgets.
- Transcripts: unknown — Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.
- Model Selection: full — 30+ documented providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, AWS Bedrock, Azure, GCP Vertex, OpenRouter (200+ models), Ollama, LM Studio, and any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
- Approval Mode: full — Granular auto-approve per category: Read, Edit, Execute, Browser, MCP. YOLO mode for everything. --auto-approve flag.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Sandbox mode via CLINE_SANDBOX env var and CLINE_SANDBOX_DATA_DIR for isolated session storage. No containerization.
- Resume: full — Full session resume via --id flag. cline history lists all sessions. Checkpoints enable rollback.
- Continue: partial — --continue flag exists but is tied to --worktree. No standalone 'continue latest' flag. Pipe-chaining supported.
- Non Interactive: full — Full headless mode. Auto-triggered with --json flag, piped stdin, or redirected stdout. Works in CI/CD.
- Output Format: full — Full JSON output via --json flag. Newline-delimited JSON objects with type, text, ts, say/ask subtype, reasoning, partial.
- Statusline: none — No customizable status line found in documentation or CLI reference.
- Telemetry: full — Anonymous telemetry with user toggle. Never captures code/file contents. Enterprise: OpenTelemetry integration (OTLP export to Datadog/Grafana/Splunk).
- Hosted Agent: none — Runs locally as VS Code extension or CLI.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint supported. 30+ documented providers. BYOK model.
- Pricing: Free (Apache-2.0), Enterprise custom pricing
- Notes: Cline has the most comprehensive feature set of any coding agent in this matrix — 17/20 fields at full support. Open source (Apache 2.0), originally 'Claude Dev'. Started as VS Code extension, expanded to CLI and SDK. 8M+ installs.

### OpenAI Codex CLI
- Docs: https://developers.openai.com/codex/
- GitHub: https://github.com/openai/codex
- Website: https://openai.com/codex/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK, Web, Mac App
- Released: Apr '25
- Latest update: Jun '26
- Some Free Usage: full — Codex pricing documents limited access included on the ChatGPT Free plan.
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: full — Codex CLI can authenticate with a ChatGPT plan instead of only an API key flow.
- Rules: full — Uses AGENTS.md instructions. Location: `AGENTS.md` in repo tree, plus user/project Codex config for defaults.
- Skills: full — Supports SKILL.md-based skills. Location: `.codex/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, `~/.codex/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, or installed plugin skill folders.
- Hooks: full — Supports Codex hooks.
- Mcp Servers: full — Supports MCP servers. Location: `~/.codex/config.toml` under MCP/server configuration.
- Custom Commands: partial — CLI supports slash-like workflows through prompts and tooling, but not a first-class command folder in this matrix. Location: no separate command folder documented; command-like workflows are provided by prompts, skills, plugins, and CLI slash commands.
- Goal Command: full — Built-in `/goal` sets a persistent thread objective with pause, resume, view, and clear; Codex keeps working until the goal is met or you stop it. Enable with `features.goals` in config if missing from the slash menu.
- Subagents: full — Codex supports sub-agent/delegation workflows in agent contexts.
- Transcripts: full — Location: Codex session event streams are JSONL under `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-<iso>-<id>.jsonl`.
- Model Selection: full — CLI accepts model configuration/flags.
- Approval Mode: full — Approval policy can be configured.
- Sandbox Mode: full — Filesystem sandbox modes can be configured.
- Resume: full — Supports resume.
- Continue: full — Supports continuing a previous session through resume/latest-session workflows.
- Non Interactive: full — Supports exec/headless use.
- Output Format: full — CLI supports structured output modes.
- Statusline: full — Supports statusline configuration in local CLI settings.
- Telemetry: full — Telemetry settings are documented in the CLI repository.
- Hosted Agent: full — Can run locally or on platform.openai.com managed infrastructure.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Supports custom model providers in config.
- Pricing: Free (limited), Plus $20/mo, Pro $100-200/mo, Business pay-as-you-go, API usage-based

### Cohere North
- Docs: https://cohere.com/north/agent-studio
- Website: https://cohere.com/north
- Form factor: SDK, Web
- Released: Jan '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Some Free Usage: blank
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: partial — North supports enterprise guardrails, but not repo-local instruction files. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Skills: partial — No-code custom agents and reusable workflows, not code-agent SKILL.md folders. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Hooks: full — Multi-step workflow automations are a core Agent Studio feature.
- Mcp Servers: full — Agent Studio explicitly mentions MCP integration. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Custom Commands: none — No coding-agent slash-command surface found. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Goal Command: none — North is a hosted agent studio with automations/goals in the product sense, not a terminal `/goal` slash command.
- Subagents: full — Agent-to-agent coordination is listed as an extensibility feature.
- Transcripts: partial — Location: hosted/web/API-managed session history; no local transcript directory documented.
- Model Selection: partial — Cohere model stack is selected through North/Cohere platform, not arbitrary coding-agent CLI flags.
- Approval Mode: partial — Enterprise guardrails and human-agent collaboration; no per-shell-command approval model.
- Sandbox Mode: full — Private deployment options include VPC, on-prem, or Cohere Model Vault.
- Resume: partial — North has memory-oriented agent workflows, but no coding-session resume command.
- Continue: partial — Memory supports cross-session workflows in North; not a CLI continue feature.
- Non Interactive: partial — API-based workflows can automate tasks, but not local repo headless coding.
- Output Format: partial — Cohere APIs return structured responses; North UI outputs are workflow-specific.
- Statusline: none — Enterprise web workspace, no CLI statusline.
- Telemetry: partial — Audit-ready logs and traceability are documented; telemetry opt-out details are not.
- Hosted Agent: full — North is an enterprise agent platform/workspace.
- Custom Model Provider: none — Built around Cohere models/platform deployment, not arbitrary model providers.
- Pricing: Enterprise/demo-based
- Notes: Included because the request mentioned Cohere. This is an enterprise agent platform with coding-capable Command A+ underneath, not a direct Claude Code/Codex-style coding agent.

### Cursor
- Docs: https://docs.cursor.com/
- Website: https://cursor.com/
- Form factor: CLI, IDE, SDK
- Released: 2023
- Latest update: May '26
- Some Free Usage: full — Cursor pricing documents a Hobby Free plan with limited Agent requests and Tab completions.
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: full — Supports project and user rules. Location: `.cursor/rules/*.mdc` in a project; user/global rules are configured in Cursor settings.
- Skills: full — Supports skills. Location: Cursor rules/agents features, not a SKILL.md folder standard in this row.
- Hooks: unknown — No lifecycle hooks documented for IDE or CLI.
- Mcp Servers: full — Supports MCP servers in both IDE and CLI. Location: `.cursor/mcp.json` for project MCP servers or user-level Cursor MCP settings.
- Custom Commands: full — CLI exposes full command controls; IDE has reusable rule/workflow patterns. Location: custom command support is through Cursor features/SDK rather than a repo command folder.
- Goal Command: none — Cursor Agent CLI documents `/plan`, `/ask`, and `/resume` slash commands but no built-in `/goal` for persistent completion conditions.
- Subagents: partial — Agent tasks are supported; first-class named subagents not modeled like Claude Code.
- Transcripts: full — Location: Cursor CLI transcripts live under `~/.cursor/projects/<slug>/agent-transcripts/<agentId>/<agentId>.jsonl`; Cursor IDE composer data is in SQLite `~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb`.
- Model Selection: full — Supports model settings in IDE and CLI.
- Approval Mode: full — Agent modes and CLI yolo/force controls expose approval controls.
- Sandbox Mode: full — CLI exposes sandbox controls; IDE trust and agent controls provide partial sandboxing.
- Resume: full — Composer/chat history in IDE and CLI resume/continue workflows support session resumption.
- Continue: full — Supports --continue in CLI and conversation history in IDE.
- Non Interactive: full — CLI supports print/headless mode; IDE is interactive only.
- Output Format: partial — CLI has partial structured output support; IDE has none.
- Statusline: partial — Editor status bar customization inherited from VS Code, not an agent-specific statusline.
- Telemetry: full — Cursor documents privacy settings.
- Hosted Agent: full — CLI agent can execute in Cursor's cloud runtime.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Supports custom API keys/models in IDE and CLI.
- Pricing: Pro $20, Pro+ $60, Ultra $200/mo
- Notes: Cursor is a VS Code-derived AI IDE with a companion CLI (cursor-agent) for headless/CI use and @cursor/sdk for programmatic local/cloud agents.

### Devin
- Docs: https://docs.devin.ai/
- Website: https://devin.ai/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK, Web
- Released: Dec '24
- Latest update: Feb '26
- Some Free Usage: full — Cognition announced a Free self-serve Devin plan with limited access.
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: full — AGENTS.md files in repos, Knowledge (persistent org-level instructions with trigger descriptions), Skills & Rules settings. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Skills: full — Full Agent Skills standard support. SKILL.md files in .agents/skills/. Supports YAML frontmatter, dynamic $ARGUMENTS and !command blocks. Also Playbooks system. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Hooks: full — Automations engine: Slack/GitHub/Linear/schedule/cron/webhook triggers. Actions include start session, message session, triage. Payload filtering and secret auth.
- Mcp Servers: full — MCP Marketplace with 40+ pre-built integrations. Supports STDIO, SSE, and HTTP transports. Custom MCP server tools for session/playbook/knowledge management. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Custom Commands: full — Built-in slash commands (/plan, /review, /test, /think-hard, /implement) plus org-level custom slash commands. Also @skills:name syntax and macros. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Goal Command: none — Devin supports team custom slash commands; no documented `/goal` for persistent thread-level objectives with auto-continuation.
- Subagents: full — Yes, called 'Managed Devins'. Coordinator spawns child Devin sessions in isolated VMs for parallel work. Auto-triage also spawns sub-devins.
- Transcripts: partial — Location: hosted/web/API-managed session history; no local transcript directory documented.
- Model Selection: unknown — Not explicitly user-facing. Devin offers 'Fast Mode' (~2x faster at 4x ACU). No documentation about letting users choose the underlying LLM model.
- Approval Mode: partial — 'Secure Mode' (disables native deployment, restricts outbound access). ACU caps, RBAC, PR review approval. No granular approval per action.
- Sandbox Mode: full — Full isolation. Every session gets a fresh ephemeral snapshot. Enterprise VPC deployment option. VPN configuration supported.
- Resume: full — API auto-resumes suspended sessions. CLI /resume alias. PR Resuming feature (Apr 2026). Archive/unarchive with undo.
- Continue: full — CLI /handoff transfers to cloud Devin with full context (git branch + conversation history). API session messaging for continuation.
- Non Interactive: full — Full headless/API mode. REST API with service user auth. Automations run fully autonomously (CI fixers, scheduled sessions, auto-triage).
- Output Format: partial — API returns structured JSON. Sessions support structured output schemas. No documented machine-readable mode for the CLI itself.
- Statusline: none — No statusline feature found in any documentation. Web app shows session status indicators but no CLI statusline.
- Telemetry: unknown — No user-configurable telemetry setting found in documentation. Enterprise audit logs available. Privacy controls mentioned but no opt-in/opt-out toggle.
- Hosted Agent: full — Primarily cloud-hosted at app.devin.ai; Devin CLI bridges to cloud agent.
- Custom Model Provider: unknown — Cerebras mentioned as enterprise-ready inference provider. No self-serve BYO model option documented.
- Pricing: Free, Pro $20/mo, Max $200/mo, Teams $80+/mo
- Notes: Devin pioneered the autonomous AI software engineer category. Focuses on enterprise: VM isolation, managed sub-agents, automations. Higher-end pricing reflects the hosted, managed approach. Windsurf acquisition announced April 2026.

### Factory Droid
- Docs: https://docs.factory.ai/reference/cli-reference
- Website: https://factory.ai/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, Web
- Released: 2025
- Latest update: May '26
- Some Free Usage: blank
- No Account Required: partial — Droid BYOK supports local models, but normal Droid CLI setup still documents Factory authentication, so account-free use is not confirmed end-to-end.
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: full — Droid BYOK supports external provider keys, open-source providers, and local models.
- Rules: full — AGENTS.md appears in Factory configuration docs. Location: `AGENTS.md` and `.factory/` project configuration.
- Skills: full — Skills can be managed and invoked from the CLI. Location: Factory Skills configuration in project/user Factory config; exact folder varies by scope.
- Hooks: full — Lifecycle hooks are first-class slash-command managed features.
- Mcp Servers: full — Supports stdio and HTTP MCP servers, registry browsing, env vars, and headers. Location: user `~/.factory/mcp.json` and project MCP configuration.
- Custom Commands: full — Custom slash commands are directly managed in the CLI. Location: Factory custom slash-command configuration in project/user Factory config.
- Goal Command: none — Factory documents custom slash commands via `/commands`; no built-in `/goal` for persistent objectives.
- Subagents: full — Custom Droids are documented as subagents.
- Transcripts: partial — Location: local searchable Droid session data is managed by Factory CLI; exact transcript database path is not documented in the CLI reference.
- Model Selection: full — Model IDs can be selected with -m/--model and /model.
- Approval Mode: full — Tiered autonomy levels control action permissions.
- Sandbox Mode: full — Docs include Sandbox Beta and unsafe mode guidance for disposable containers.
- Resume: full — Resume by session ID or last modified session.
- Continue: full — Exec can continue an existing session; interactive defaults to the last modified session.
- Non Interactive: full — droid exec is the headless automation mode.
- Output Format: full — Search supports JSON output; API and readiness endpoints expose structured data.
- Statusline: full — Custom status line is documented.
- Telemetry: unknown — Analytics/compliance APIs exist, but no user telemetry opt-out setting found.
- Hosted Agent: partial — Factory App and Droid Computers exist; CLI can register machines and run daemon/server flows.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Bring Your Own Key/custom model path is documented.
- Pricing: Factory plans; usage details in Factory pricing
- Notes: One of the most feature-dense CLI surfaces in this update: autonomy levels, worktrees, custom droids, plugins, MCP, statusline, skills, hooks, and headless exec.

### Gemini CLI
- Docs: https://google-gemini.github.io/gemini-cli/
- GitHub: https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli
- Website: https://blog.google/technology/developers/introducing-gemini-cli-open-source-ai-agent/
- Form factor: CLI
- Status: Deprecated
- Released: Jun '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Some Free Usage: full — Gemini CLI documents a free tier when signing in with a Google account.
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: full — Gemini CLI can use quotas from Gemini Code Assist editions, and also supports API-key pay-as-you-go.
- Rules: full — Uses GEMINI.md context files. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Skills: partial — Extensible through tools/extensions rather than the same SKILL.md contract. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Hooks: full — Supports hooks in settings.
- Mcp Servers: full — Supports MCP servers. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Custom Commands: full — Supports commands. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Goal Command: none — Deprecated Gemini CLI surface; no documented `/goal`. Google points CLI users to Antigravity, which also lacks a `/goal` command in public docs.
- Subagents: unknown
- Transcripts: unknown — Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.
- Model Selection: full — Model can be configured.
- Approval Mode: full — Approval mode is configurable.
- Sandbox Mode: full — Sandbox settings are documented.
- Resume: full — Supports resume workflows.
- Continue: full — Supports continuing a previous session through resume/latest-session workflows.
- Non Interactive: full — Supports prompt/headless use.
- Output Format: partial
- Statusline: unknown
- Telemetry: full — Telemetry is documented.
- Hosted Agent: none — Local CLI tool backed by Google Gemini API.
- Custom Model Provider: partial — Primarily Gemini/Google-oriented provider configuration.
- Pricing: Free (Apache-2.0), Gemini API usage billed separately
- Notes: Deprecated: Google points new CLI work to Antigravity. Kept as an explicit agent-launch option, but excluded from random selection in this repo. Headless/scripting mode only; no published agent embed SDK.

### GitHub Copilot
- Docs: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/concepts/coding-agent/coding-agent
- GitHub: https://github.com/features/copilot
- Website: https://github.com/features/copilot
- Form factor: CLI, IDE, Extension, SDK, Web
- Released: Jun '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Some Free Usage: blank
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: full — Multiple layers: .github/copilot-instructions.md (repo-wide), path-specific .github/instructions/**, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, personal instructions, org-level. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Skills: full — Open Agent Skills standard. .github/skills, .claude/skills, .agents/skills. Personal skills in ~/.copilot/skills. gh skill CLI for discovery/install. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Hooks: full — Full lifecycle hook system: sessionStart, sessionEnd, userPromptSubmitted, preToolUse (approve/deny), postToolUse, agentStop, subagentStop, errorOccurred. .github/hooks/*.json.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full MCP in cloud agent, CLI, and IDE. Supports local, stdio, http, sse transports. Built-in GitHub MCP server. Enterprise allowlist controls. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Custom Commands: partial — Built-in slash commands in IDE (/fix, /tests, /explain, /help) and CLI (/delegate, /pr, /resume). Prompt files (*.prompt.md) for reusable snippets. No user-defined custom slash commands. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Goal Command: none — GitHub Copilot CLI has slash commands and agent modes but no `/goal` for persistent completion conditions across turns.
- Subagents: full — Subagents in VS Code and JetBrains IDE agent mode. Isolated context window. Invocation: automatic, direct, or via #runSubagent. Cannot create sub-subagents.
- Transcripts: unknown — Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.
- Model Selection: full — Models from Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, xAI. Auto model selection available. Each model has a premium request multiplier.
- Approval Mode: partial — Desktop app has Interactive, Plan, and Autopilot modes. preToolUse hook can approve/deny. No universal 'always ask before X' toggle.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Cloud agent runs in ephemeral GitHub Actions environment (natural sandbox). CLI has tool permission controls. No explicit sandbox toggle.
- Resume: full — CLI: copilot --resume opens session picker, copilot --resume SESSION-ID jumps to specific session. /resume during interactive session.
- Continue: full — CLI: copilot --continue resumes most recent session directly. IDE agent sessions persistent. Desktop app sessions in sidebar.
- Non Interactive: full — CLI programmatic/scripted usage in GitHub Actions. --non-interactive mode. The GitHub CLI extension entrypoint is `gh copilot -- ...`; standalone payload availability can depend on the extension downloader/auth state. Copilot SDK for API-based integration.
- Output Format: partial — Copilot SDK provides programmatic access with structured responses. No documented --json flag for general CLI output.
- Statusline: none — No statusline feature documented anywhere in GitHub Copilot's documentation.
- Telemetry: partial — Enterprise audit logging, agent session monitoring. Data retention: prompts retained 28 days. Users can opt out of training data. No granular telemetry UI.
- Hosted Agent: full — Copilot Cloud Agent runs autonomously in GitHub Actions-powered ephemeral environment.
- Custom Model Provider: full — BYOK in CLI: env vars for provider type, base URL, API key. OpenAI-compatible endpoints, Azure OpenAI, Anthropic, local models via Ollama.
- Pricing: Free $0, Pro $10/mo, Pro+ $39/mo, Business $19/user/mo, Enterprise $39/user/mo
- Notes: GitHub Copilot's agent capabilities span IDE, cloud, and CLI. The cloud agent is unique: runs in ephemeral GitHub Actions environment. Desktop app (preview) adds parallel agent sessions. Ecosystem is rapidly evolving with weekly feature drops.

### Grok Build
- Docs: https://x.ai/news/grok-build-cli
- Website: https://x.ai/news/grok-build-cli
- Form factor: CLI
- Released: May '26
- Latest update: May '26
- Some Free Usage: blank
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: full — Official launch page says AGENTS.md works out of the box. Location: `AGENTS.md` in repo tree.
- Skills: full — Skills and plugin marketplace are called out in the launch page. Location: plugin/skill folders managed by Grok Build plugin marketplace; exact disk root not yet public.
- Hooks: full — Hooks are explicitly listed as supported.
- Mcp Servers: full — MCP servers work out of the box according to xAI. Location: MCP server configuration picked up from the repo/tooling; exact disk root not yet public.
- Custom Commands: unknown — Slash command UX exists, but public docs do not yet show user-defined commands. Location: custom command folder not documented; slash UX exists for built-ins like `/feedback`.
- Goal Command: none — Grok Build launch materials mention plugins, hooks, and skills but not a `/goal` slash command.
- Subagents: full — Delegates larger tasks to specialized parallel subagents, with worktree integration.
- Transcripts: unknown — Location: transcript/session disk storage is not documented in the public beta launch page.
- Model Selection: partial — Launch screenshot references config.toml for models and API keys; model catalog details are not yet public.
- Approval Mode: full — Plan mode lets users approve, comment on, or rewrite a plan before execution.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Worktree isolation is documented for subagents; broader filesystem/network sandbox controls are not yet documented.
- Resume: unknown — No public resume/session-id documentation found in the launch page.
- Continue: unknown — No public continue-latest command documentation found.
- Non Interactive: full — Headless mode is documented as -p.
- Output Format: unknown — ACP support implies structured protocol integration, but no JSON/stdout output format docs found.
- Statusline: unknown — No user-configurable statusline documentation found.
- Telemetry: unknown — No user-visible telemetry opt-out setting found in public launch docs.
- Hosted Agent: none — Documented as a terminal CLI, not a managed hosted worker.
- Custom Model Provider: partial — Config references models and API keys, but arbitrary OpenAI-compatible provider support is not documented.
- Pricing: Included for SuperGrok and X Premium Plus subscribers during beta
- Notes: Early beta. Strong documented parity on AGENTS.md, skills, hooks, MCP, headless mode, ACP, and parallel subagents; lower confidence for session management and telemetry because public docs are still sparse.

### Google Jules
- Docs: https://jules.google/
- Website: https://jules.google/
- Form factor: Web
- Released: Aug '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Some Free Usage: blank
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: unknown — No public docs found for persistent repo instruction files. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Skills: none — No reusable skill/playbook system found. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Hooks: partial — GitHub issue label assignment acts as a lightweight trigger.
- Mcp Servers: unknown — No public MCP server configuration found. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Custom Commands: none — Prompt and issue assignment workflow, not user-defined slash commands. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Goal Command: none — Jules is a hosted async agent for GitHub issues/PRs; no interactive `/goal` slash command in public docs.
- Subagents: partial — Plan tiers advertise multi-agent development and high concurrent task counts.
- Transcripts: partial — Location: hosted/web/API-managed session history; no local transcript directory documented.
- Model Selection: partial — Tier-dependent model access; not arbitrary model selection.
- Approval Mode: full — User reviews plan/diff and approves changes/PR.
- Sandbox Mode: full — Work happens in a Cloud VM clone.
- Resume: partial — Async task history exists in the hosted product, but no explicit session resume docs found.
- Continue: partial — Designed for async continuation in hosted tasks; no CLI continue command.
- Non Interactive: partial — Can be triggered from GitHub issues, but not a general local headless CLI.
- Output Format: partial — Outputs plans, diffs, and PRs; no general machine-readable format found.
- Statusline: none — Hosted UI, no CLI statusline.
- Telemetry: unknown — No product-specific telemetry toggle found.
- Hosted Agent: full — Hosted async agent running in Google-managed Cloud VMs.
- Custom Model Provider: none — Uses Gemini models; BYO provider not documented.
- Pricing: Free 15 tasks/day; Pro 100 tasks/day; Ultra 300 tasks/day
- Notes: Best categorized as an async hosted coding worker rather than a terminal pair-programmer. Strong on GitHub PR flow, approvals, Cloud VM isolation, and concurrency.

- Latest update: May '26
