- Skills: unknown — No reusable skill folder standard found. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Hooks: none — No lifecycle hook system found.
- Mcp Servers: unknown — No Junie-specific MCP configuration found in public docs reviewed. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Custom Commands: unknown — No custom slash-command system found. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Goal Command: none — Junie runs as an IDE agent with tasks and modes; no built-in `/goal` slash command documented.
- Subagents: unknown — No first-class subagent docs found.
- Transcripts: unknown — Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.
- Model Selection: partial — Enterprise docs mention JetBrains AI service plus selected OpenAI/Bedrock models.
- Approval Mode: full — Product page lists autonomous, user-assisted, and semi-auto modes.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Runs inside JetBrains IDE context; no explicit standalone sandbox controls found.
- Resume: partial — IDE tasks have progress/history, but explicit session resume docs are limited.
- Continue: partial — Interactive IDE task continuation is available; no CLI continue command confirmed.
- Non Interactive: unknown — CLI exists and is documented for terminal use, but no verified headless automation flag was found in docs reviewed.
- Output Format: unknown — No machine-readable output mode found.
- Statusline: none — IDE UI, no custom CLI statusline found.
- Telemetry: unknown — No Junie-specific telemetry toggle found in docs reviewed.
- Hosted Agent: none — Primarily an IDE agent rather than a managed cloud worker.
- Custom Model Provider: partial — Enterprise service supports selected provider/model options, not arbitrary endpoints.
- Pricing: Bundled with JetBrains AI tiers/free credits
- Notes: Strong fit for JetBrains users, especially debugger-aware workflows. Public docs expose less extensibility detail than CLI-first agents. macOS installs may involve app-bundle/shim setup rather than a simple standalone binary.

### Kilo Code
- Docs: https://kilo.ai/docs/getting-started
- GitHub: https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode
- Website: https://kilo.ai/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension
- Released: Feb '26
- Latest update: Jun '26
- Some Free Usage: full — Kilo Code documents free/open-source access plus Auto Free/BYOK/local inference options.
- No Account Required: full — Can run local models via Ollama, LM Studio, or Atomic Chat without a Kilo-hosted account.
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: full — Kilo documents use of an existing ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription and BYOK/local provider options.
- Rules: full — Full support: Project Rules (via instructions key in kilo.jsonc), Global Rules (same in ~/.config/kilo/kilo.jsonc), AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, CONTEXT.md. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Skills: full — Full Agent Skills standard. SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter. Global ~/.kilo/skills/, project .kilo/skills/. Compat with .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Hooks: full — Full support via Plugin system. Lifecycle hooks: config, event (bus events), tool hooks (execute.before/after), chat hooks, permission hooks, provider hooks. 17+ hook types.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full MCP support. Local (STDIO) and remote (HTTP Streamable) transports. OAuth 2.0. Tool permissions via allow/ask/deny. Built-in Playwright MCP. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Custom Commands: full — Full support via Workflows (slash commands). Markdown files in ~/.config/kilo/commands/ or .kilo/commands/. YAML frontmatter with description, agent, model. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Goal Command: none — Kilo Code uses custom workflows/slash commands; no first-party `/goal` for persistent session objectives.
- Subagents: full — Full subagent support. Custom modes/agents with mode property: primary, subagent, or all. Per-agent prompts, models, permissions, temperature. task tool spawns sub-agents.
- Transcripts: unknown — Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.
- Model Selection: full — 500+ models via 15+ providers. Switch mid-session with /models. Per-agent model pinning. CLI flags: -m, --model. Auto model routing.
- Approval Mode: full — Three-tier: allow (auto-approve), ask (prompt), deny (block). Glob patterns. --auto flag for autonomous mode. Per-agent permission overrides.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — No dedicated Docker/sandbox isolation. External_directory permissions control reads/writes outside workspace. Permission system with allow/deny/ask.
- Resume: full — Session continuation via --continue/-c flag. -s, --session <id> for specific sessions. --fork to fork existing sessions. /sessions slash command.
- Continue: full — --continue/-c flag on kilo, kilo run, and kilo attach commands. /sessions slash command also accepts /continue alias.
- Non Interactive: full — Multiple non-interactive modes: kilo run <message> (one-shot), --auto flag (fully autonomous), --json (JSON output), --json-io (bidirectional JSON). kilo serve for headless server.
- Output Format: full — --format flag with choices default (TUI) and json (raw JSON events). --json flag implies auto mode. --json-io for bidirectional JSON. Session export/import.
- Statusline: none — No customizable statusline feature found. Has /status slash command and /themes for theming, but no persistent status line customization.
- Telemetry: full — Enabled by default. Disable via experimental.openTelemetry: false. OpenTelemetry export when OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set.
- Hosted Agent: partial — Cloud Agents feature allows web-based sessions; primary use is local via extension/CLI.
- Custom Model Provider: full — BYOK for 15+ providers. Plugin system for entirely custom providers. Local models supported. 500+ models via Kilo Gateway at zero markup.
- Pricing: Free (MIT), Kilo Pass $19-199/mo, Teams $15/user/mo
- Notes: Kilo Code is a rapidly evolving open-source coding agent (MIT, 19k+ stars). Originally forked from Continue, now independent. Notable for extensive plugin system, 500+ model support through Kilo Gateway, and cross-surface session sync.

### Kimi CLI
- Docs: https://www.kimi.com/code/docs/en/kimi-code-cli/getting-started.html
- GitHub: https://github.com/MoonshotAI/kimi-cli
- Website: https://kimi.moonshot.cn/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK
- Released: Oct '25
- Latest update: Jun '26
- Some Free Usage: blank
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: full — Full AGENTS.md support. Hierarchical loading from git root to working dir, including .kimi/AGENTS.md at each level (32 KiB budget). /init command auto-generates AGENTS.md. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Skills: full — Full Agent Skills format (SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter). Four-tier auto-discovery. Cross-compatible with Claude/Codex skill dirs. Flow skills with Mermaid/D2 flowcharts. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Hooks: partial — Supported (Beta). 13 lifecycle events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, SessionEnd, SubagentStart/Stop, PreCompact, PostCompact, Notification. Shell commands via stdin.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full MCP support. Transport: streamable HTTP, stdio, OAuth. kimi mcp add/list/test/remove/auth commands. Config in ~/.kimi/mcp.json. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Custom Commands: partial — Custom plugins (Beta) via plugin.json with executable scripts. Slash commands (/skill:, /flow:) serve as custom invocation. No arbitrary custom aliases in config. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Goal Command: none — Kimi Code CLI docs cover core operations and plugins but not a `/goal` command.
- Subagents: full — Full subagent support. Built-in types: coder, explore, plan. YAML agent files with inheritance. Agent tool with subagent_type, model, resume, run_in_background params.
- Transcripts: unknown — Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.
- Model Selection: full — Full model selection. Provider types: kimi, openai_legacy, openai_responses, anthropic, gemini, vertexai. /model interactive picker. CLI flags: --model, --thinking.
- Approval Mode: full — Full system: Normal (approval with diff preview), YOLO mode (--yolo, auto-approves all), AFK mode (--afk, auto-approves and dismisses questions). Status bar badges.
- Sandbox Mode: none — No dedicated sandbox/mode. Has workspace scope boundaries, file operation approval, sensitive file protection (.env, SSH keys). No containerized isolation.
- Resume: full — Full session resume. --session/--resume/-S/-r with optional session ID. Interactive picker. Auto-restores approval decisions, plan mode, subagent instances.
- Continue: full — kimi --continue/-C resumes most recent session in current working directory. /new for fresh session. /sessions lists and switches between sessions.
- Non Interactive: full — --print flag for print mode: auto-exit after prompt, implicit auto-approval (AFK behavior), text stdout. --quiet shorthand. Exit codes: 0=success, 1=error, 75=retryable.
- Output Format: full — --input-format: text or stream-json (JSONL). --output-format: text or stream-json (JSONL). --final-message-only flag. --quiet shorthand. Unified Message format for JSON I/O.
- Statusline: full — Full status bar in shell mode: context usage %, mode indicators, YOLO/AFK badges, plan mode badge, background task counts, provider display name.
- Telemetry: unknown — No explicit telemetry documentation found. Has automatic update checks (disable via KIMI_CLI_NO_AUTO_UPDATE), but no stated telemetry/analytics collection policy.
- Hosted Agent: none — Local CLI tool backed by Moonshot API.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Full custom provider support. providers table in config.toml with type, base_url, api_key, custom_headers. Six provider types including OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Gemini.
- Pricing: Membership (credits) or BYOK
- Notes: Kimi CLI by Moonshot AI (Beijing) is an Apache-2.0 open-source coding agent with rapid development pace (97 releases in 7 months). Strong AGENTS.md, skills, MCP, subagent, and YOLO/AFK approval mode support. Notable for status bar with badges and comprehensive JSON streaming output.

### Kiro
- Docs: https://kiro.dev/docs/
- Website: https://kiro.dev/
- Form factor: CLI, IDE, Web
- Released: Jul '25
- Latest update: May '26
- Some Free Usage: full — Kiro billing docs say new users start on the perpetual Kiro Free tier.
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: full — Steering files provide project/team instructions. Location: `.kiro/steering/` project steering files and global steering settings.
- Skills: full — Kiro CLI references agent skills and Powers. Location: Kiro Powers use `POWER.md`; agent skills/knowledge are managed in Kiro project configuration.
- Hooks: full — Smart hooks are documented as core CLI features.
- Mcp Servers: full — Native MCP support in IDE and CLI. Location: `.kiro/mcp.json` / Kiro MCP configuration.
- Custom Commands: full — CLI has slash commands and prompt/custom-agent workflows. Location: CLI slash commands are built in; custom agents/prompts are managed in Kiro custom-agent configuration.
- Goal Command: none — Kiro CLI documents slash-style commands but no built-in `/goal` for persistent objectives.
- Subagents: full — Built-in subagents for context gathering and parallel general-purpose tasks.
- Transcripts: partial — Location: Kiro CLI uses directory-based conversation persistence; exact transcript directory is not specified in the public docs reviewed.
- Model Selection: full — CLI /model displays and selects available models.
- Approval Mode: full — Custom agents can use predefined tool permissions and approved plans.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Permissioning is documented; explicit local filesystem/network sandbox modes are not clear.
- Resume: full — Directory-based conversation persistence and /save /load.
- Continue: full — kiro chat preserves project conversation history for continuing work.
- Non Interactive: full — Headless --print examples are documented for CI/CD automation.
- Output Format: partial — Can export conversation state to JSON; no broad JSON stream mode found.
- Statusline: unknown — No user-configurable statusline docs found.
- Telemetry: unknown — No telemetry control found in the docs reviewed.
- Hosted Agent: partial — Kiro Web exists, but the public CLI docs emphasize local terminal operation.
- Custom Model Provider: none — Docs describe Kiro's model set/Auto agent, not arbitrary model providers.
- Pricing: Included in Kiro pricing tiers
- Notes: Spec/steering-first agentic IDE plus CLI. Particularly strong on steering, hooks, custom agents, ACP-compatible IDEs, and CI automation.

### OpenCode
- Docs: https://opencode.ai/docs/
- GitHub: https://github.com/sst/opencode
- Website: https://opencode.ai/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK, Mac App
- Released: 2025
- Latest update: Jun '26
- Some Free Usage: full — OpenCode homepage documents free models included.
- No Account Required: full — OpenCode supports running local models, so it can be used without an OpenCode-hosted account when configured locally.
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: full — OpenCode supports 75+ LLM providers and local models via provider configuration/API keys.
- Rules: full — Supports instruction/config files. Location: `AGENTS.md` and opencode config files in the repo/user config scope.
- Skills: full — Supports skills. Location: opencode Agent Skills are stored as skill folders with `SKILL.md` according to the configured scope.
- Hooks: full — Config supports commands/hooks workflows.
- Mcp Servers: full — Supports MCP servers. Location: `opencode.json` / opencode config MCP server entries.
- Custom Commands: full — Supports commands. Location: opencode custom commands are configured under opencode command/config files, not shell aliases.
- Goal Command: none — Built-in TUI commands are `/init`, `/undo`, `/redo`, `/share`, and `/help` only; no first-party `/goal` for persistent session objectives yet.
- Subagents: full — Supports custom agents.
- Transcripts: full — Location: OpenCode stores sessions/messages in SQLite `~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db`; legacy storage uses `~/.local/share/opencode/storage/session|message|part/`.
- Model Selection: full — Supports provider/model selection.
- Approval Mode: full — Permission controls are configurable.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Permission model controls tool access.
- Resume: full — Supports session options.
- Continue: full — Supports --continue and explicit session selection.
- Non Interactive: full — Supports run mode.
- Output Format: full — CLI supports format options.
- Statusline: unknown
- Telemetry: full — Config includes telemetry-related settings.
- Hosted Agent: none — Runs entirely locally.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Supports many providers and custom provider configuration.
- Pricing: Free (MIT, BYOK), Go $10/mo (first month $5), Zen pay-per-token (min $20), Enterprise per-seat

### Pi
- Docs: https://pi.dev/docs/latest
- GitHub: https://github.com/earendil-works/pi
- Website: https://pi.dev
- Form factor: CLI, SDK
- Released: May '26
- Latest update: Jun '26
- Some Free Usage: blank
- No Account Required: full — Pi supports custom providers/models including Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, and proxies, so local usage need not require a Pi-hosted account.
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: full — Pi documents subscription-based providers via OAuth plus API-key providers and auth-file/env configuration.
- Rules: full — Loads `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` from `~/.pi/agent/`, ancestors, and cwd. Supports global and project context files with path-based precedence.
- Skills: full — Discovers skills from `~/.pi/agent/skills/`, `~/.agents/skills/`, `.pi/skills/`, and `.agents/skills/`, including `SKILL.md` directories. Supports `/skill:name` command registration and cross-harness imports (e.g. Claude/ Codex).
- Hooks: full — Extension API supports lifecycle/tool hooks via TypeScript extensions, including tool event hooks and custom event handlers. Full extension-based hook model from docs and examples.
- Mcp Servers: none — README explicitly states no MCP. MCP-like capabilities can be built via extensions instead.
- Custom Commands: full — Rich slash-command UI includes built-ins like `/model`, `/resume`, `/tree`, `/fork`, `/clone`, `/compact`, `/export`, `/share`, `/settings`, and `/login`.
- Goal Command: none — No `/goal` concept is documented; README is explicit about what is *not* built in (including plan mode and no-permission prompts).
- Subagents: none — README says "No sub-agents." suggests using spawned pi instances/context tools instead of native subagent orchestration.
- Transcripts: full — Sessions are saved automatically to `~/.pi/agent/sessions/` with session IDs and `/session` command showing file and metadata.
- Model Selection: full — Model/provider selection is fully exposed with `/model` in interactive mode and `--provider`, `--model`, `--models` CLI options.
- Approval Mode: none — Philosophy page says there are no permission popups by default and no approval mode is documented as a first-class control.
- Sandbox Mode: none — No sandbox mode setting is documented; README points users to tmux/container-like patterns instead of built-in sandbox controls.
- Resume: full — Sessions can be resumed with `pi -r/--resume`, `--session`, and interactive `/resume` + `/fork`/`/clone` branch flow.
- Continue: full — Continue latest session with `-c/--continue`; also supports session browsing and tree-based continuation commands in-session.
- Non Interactive: full — Supports print mode (`-p/--print`) and machine-friendly transports (`--mode json`, `--mode rpc`) for scripted/CI usage.
- Output Format: full — `--mode json` outputs JSON lines and JSON mode docs exist for event streaming consumers and integrations.
- Statusline: none — Interactive UI includes a built-in footer/status area but no documented customization API for a user-defined status line.
- Telemetry: partial — Telemetry is configurable via `enableInstallTelemetry` and can be disabled; `PI_SKIP_VERSION_CHECK` and offline modes control network checks.
- Hosted Agent: none — CLI is local-first; no dedicated hosted runtime platform is documented.
- Custom Model Provider: full — Supports custom provider/API integration through `~/.pi/agent/models.json` and custom provider registration hooks for OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Google, and custom flows.
- Pricing: Free (MIT)
- Notes: Pi is a minimal terminal coding harness with extensibility-first defaults (extensions, skills, prompt templates, themes, packages), plus SDK and RPC/event-stream integrations.

### Qwen Code
- Docs: https://qwenlm.github.io/qwen-code-docs/en/users/overview
- GitHub: https://github.com/QwenLM/qwen-code
- Website: https://qwenlm.github.io/qwen-code-docs/
- Form factor: CLI, Extension, SDK
- Released: Jun '25
- Latest update: Jun '26
- Some Free Usage: blank
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: full — Hierarchical context files (QWEN.md) at project/user levels. Custom system prompts via --system-prompt and --append-system-prompt. Layered config across system/user/project/env/cli. Location: Qwen Code project/user instruction files and extension packages; commonly `AGENTS.md`-style repo context plus Qwen config.
- Skills: full — Full Agent Skills system: SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter, model-invoked, personal (~/.qwen/skills/) and project (.qwen/skills/), path gating, extension skills. Location: Qwen Code extensions package skills via `qwen-extension.json`.
- Hooks: full — 14 hook events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, SessionEnd, SubagentStart/Stop, PreCompact, PostCompact, Notification, PermissionRequest. Three executors: command, http, function.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full MCP: stdio, SSE, HTTP transports. OAuth 2.0, tool filtering, server trust, global allow/deny lists. CLI management via qwen mcp commands. Location: Qwen Code MCP settings in Qwen config/extension packages.
- Custom Commands: full — Full custom commands: Markdown files in ~/.qwen/commands/ (global) and .qwen/commands/ (project). Support {{args}}, !{shell}, @{file} references. Namespace support. Location: Qwen Code extensions can package custom commands via `qwen-extension.json`.
- Goal Command: none — Qwen Code supports custom shortcut commands; no built-in `/goal` for auto-continuing until a completion condition.
- Subagents: full — Full subagent system: named subagents with YAML frontmatter, fork subagents (context-inheriting), per-agent model/permission/tool config, extension subagents.
- Transcripts: unknown — Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.
- Model Selection: full — Full model switching via /model. Supports OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Gemini protocols. Local models via Ollama/vLLM. Per-model reasoning config.
- Approval Mode: full — Four modes: Plan (read-only), Default (manual approval), Auto-Edit (auto file edits, manual shell), YOLO (auto all). Shift+Tab to cycle.
- Sandbox Mode: full — Full sandboxing: macOS Seatbelt (sandbox-exec with 6 profiles) and Docker/Podman containers (full isolation, custom images, network proxying, UID/GID mapping).
- Resume: full — /resume command in interactive mode. --resume <sessionId> in headless mode. Session data as JSONL under ~/.qwen/projects/.
- Continue: full — --continue flag resumes the most recent session for the current project in headless mode.
- Non Interactive: full — -p/--prompt for headless. stdin piping, JSON output, resume/continue, persistent retry mode for CI. GitHub Actions integration.
- Output Format: full — Three formats: text (default), json (structured array), stream-json (line-delimited, real-time). --include-partial-messages for granular streaming.
- Statusline: full — Custom status line via ui.statusLine in settings.json. Shell command receives JSON context. Multi-line support, hot reload, refreshInterval for timer-based updates.
- Telemetry: full — OpenTelemetry-based: traces, logs, metrics. Export to local file, OTLP collector, Alibaba Cloud Managed Service. Privacy opt-out via privacy.usageStatisticsEnabled.
- Hosted Agent: partial — Can use Alibaba Cloud Coding Plan for hosted model access; agent runs locally.
- Custom Model Provider: full — modelProviders config with openai/anthropic/gemini auth types. Local models via any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio). OpenRouter, Fireworks AI.
- Pricing: Free (Apache-2.0)
- Notes: Qwen Code by Alibaba (QwenLM) is the most feature-complete open-source coding agent in this matrix — 20/20 fields at full (tied with Cline). Standout features: macOS Seatbelt + Docker sandbox, OpenTelemetry telemetry, custom status line, three output formats. Apache-2.0, 24.6k stars.

### Replit Agent
- Docs: https://docs.replit.com/replitai/agent
- Website: https://replit.com/ai
- Form factor: Web
- Released: Sep '24
- Latest update: Sep '25
- Some Free Usage: blank
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: partial — Project context exists inside Replit, but no AGENTS.md-style standard confirmed. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Skills: none — No reusable skill-folder system found. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Hooks: partial — Agent 3 can run autonomous test/fix loops, but not general user-defined lifecycle hooks.
- Mcp Servers: unknown — No MCP configuration found in public Agent docs. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Custom Commands: none — Prompt/chat workflow, not user-defined slash-command folders. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.
- Goal Command: none — Replit Agent is a hosted web agent; no terminal `/goal` slash command.
- Subagents: partial — Agent 3 can build agents and run background tasks, but no first-class custom subagent framework found.
- Transcripts: partial — Location: hosted/web/API-managed session history; no local transcript directory documented.
- Model Selection: none — No user model selection for Replit Agent found.
- Approval Mode: partial — User reviews generated work in Replit; granular command approval controls are not documented.
- Sandbox Mode: full — Runs inside Replit-hosted workspaces.
- Resume: partial — Agent work persists in Replit projects, but no explicit session resume command.
- Continue: partial — Can continue in the same hosted workspace/project.
- Non Interactive: none — No public headless/CLI automation mode found.
- Output Format: none — No machine-readable output mode found.
- Statusline: none — Hosted UI, no custom CLI statusline.
- Telemetry: unknown — No Agent-specific telemetry toggle found.
- Hosted Agent: full — Hosted app-building agent in Replit.
- Custom Model Provider: none — No BYO model/provider support found.
- Pricing: Included in Replit plan/usage model
- Notes: Best for hosted app prototyping/build-deploy workflows, not for local repo terminal automation.

### Roo Code
- Docs: https://docs.roocode.com/
- GitHub: https://github.com/RooCodeInc/Roo-Code
- Website: https://roocode.com/
- Form factor: Extension
- Released: Mid-2025
- Latest update: May '26
- Some Free Usage: blank
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: full — Comprehensive: .roorules, .roo/rules/, .clinerules fallback, ~/.roo/rules/ global dir, AGENTS.md/AGENT.md support, mode-specific rules via rules-{modeSlug}. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Skills: full — Full skills (added v3.38.0, Dec 2025). Follows agentskills.io spec: SKILL.md with frontmatter, progressive discovery, mode-specific targeting, bundled resources. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Hooks: none — No lifecycle hooks system found in any documentation. Built-in slash commands exist but no user-defined lifecycle hooks.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full MCP since v2.2.0. STDIO, Streamable HTTP, SSE transports. Roo Code Marketplace for one-click MCP install. Per-tool alwaysAllow permissions. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Custom Commands: full — Full slash commands: .roo/commands/ (project) and ~/.roo/commands/ (global). Frontmatter with description, argument-hint, mode targeting. Built-in /init command. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Goal Command: none — Roo Code supports custom slash commands; no built-in `/goal` for persistent session objectives.
- Subagents: full — Full orchestration via Orchestrator Mode (Boomerang Mode). new_task tool delegates to specialized modes. Isolated context with explicit context transfer.
- Transcripts: unknown — Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.
- Model Selection: full — 22+ documented providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, DeepSeek, Google Gemini, LM Studio, Ollama, OpenRouter, and more. Sticky models per mode.
- Approval Mode: full — Granular auto-approval: read/write operations, approved commands (allowlist/denylist), MCP tools (dual permission), mode switching, subtasks, follow-up questions.
- Sandbox Mode: none — No explicit sandbox mode. Checkpoints provide git-based recovery. .rooignore for access control. No isolated/sandboxed execution environment.
- Resume: partial — Checkpoints provide 'Restore Files & Task'. CLI has --create-with-session-id and session resume/history support (v3.51.0+).
- Continue: full — CLI session resume/history support. Per-task history persists and can be resumed. History view shows past tasks for reopening.
- Non Interactive: full — CLI binary (roo) with headless mode. NDJSON stdin/stdout stream protocol. Subcommands: run, list, upgrade. Platform binaries available.
- Output Format: partial — CLI supports NDJSON stdin stream protocol for structured I/O. exitCode in tool_result events. No formal --json or --print flag.
- Statusline: none — No custom status line feature found. VS Code extension has chat panel UI but no configurable status line.
- Telemetry: full — PostHog-powered. Anonymous feature usage, VS Code machine ID, error reports. No PII/code/prompts. Opt-out available. API error telemetry per provider.
- Hosted Agent: none — Runs locally as VS Code extension or CLI. Project is now archived.
- Custom Model Provider: full — OpenAI Compatible provider for arbitrary API endpoints. LiteLLM routing. 22+ built-in providers. Debug proxy support. Model-agnostic.
- Pricing: Free (Apache-2.0)
- Notes: Roo Code was a popular Cline fork (24k stars, 574k VS Code installs) that was shut down on May 15, 2026. The repository is now archived and read-only. VS Code extension-host API only; no standalone npm agent SDK. Community forks include ZooCode and Cline.

### Windsurf Cascade
- Docs: https://docs.windsurf.com/
- Website: https://windsurf.com/
- Form factor: CLI, IDE, Extension
- Released: Nov '24
- Latest update: Apr '26
- Some Free Usage: blank
- No Account Required: blank
- Can Use Other Subscriptions: blank
- Rules: full — Three scopes: Global (~/.codeium/windsurf/memories/global_rules.md, always-on), Workspace (.windsurf/rules/*.md with frontmatter modes), System (/etc/windsurf/rules/*.md). AGENTS.md support. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Skills: full — Full support. SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter. Progressive disclosure. Scopes: Workspace .windsurf/skills/, Global ~/.codeium/windsurf/skills/. Discovers .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Hooks: full — 12 hook events: pre/post read/write code, pre/post run command, pre/post MCP tool use, pre/post user prompt, post cascade response. Pre-hooks can block with exit code 2.
- Mcp Servers: full — Full native MCP. MCP Marketplace with one-click install. stdio, Streamable HTTP, SSE transports. OAuth support. Admin whitelists for teams. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Custom Commands: full — Custom commands via Workflows (slash commands /[workflow-name]) and Command palette. Terminal has 4 auto-execution levels. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.
- Goal Command: none — Windsurf Cascade has workflows and hooks but no documented `/goal` slash command for persistent objectives.
- Subagents: full — Devin Cloud (autonomous agent on own VM), Devin Local Agent, Fast Context (specialized 20x faster retrieval), Agent Command Center manages all agents in Kanban view.
- Transcripts: unknown — Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.
- Model Selection: full — Full selection: SWE-1.6/1.5, Claude Opus/Sonnet, GPT-5.x series, Gemini, DeepSeek V4, Kimi, Grok, and more. Adaptive (intelligent router) and Arena Mode (side-by-side).
- Approval Mode: full — 4 terminal levels: Disabled (require approval), Allowlist Only, Auto (Cascade judges), Turbo (all auto-executed). Allow/deny lists. Cascade Code/Chat/Ask mode distinctions.
- Sandbox Mode: partial — Devin cloud agent runs on isolated VM. Git worktrees isolate parallel sessions. Dev Containers support. No standalone sandbox toggle for the IDE.
- Resume: full — Cascade has 'continue' button. Auto-Continue setting. Named checkpoints/snapshots for reversion. Sessions persist and switch via dropdown.
- Continue: full — Auto-Continue setting automatically continues when hitting tool-call limits. Queued messages for follow-ups while Cascade is working. Real-time editor awareness.
- Non Interactive: partial — `windsurf` command-line launcher can be installed into PATH from the IDE/desktop app. Devin CLI agent runs in terminal. No documented --non-interactive flag for the Windsurf IDE launcher.
- Output Format: partial — Enterprise analytics API available. post_cascade_response_with_transcript hook writes full conversation to JSONL files at ~/.windsurf/transcripts/{trajectory_id}.jsonl.
- Statusline: partial — No customizable status line like Cursor. Status bar has model indicator and plan/usage info. Agent Command Center is a Kanban dashboard of all agents.
- Telemetry: partial — Telemetry settings mentioned in troubleshooting docs. Enterprise analytics API. No documented granular telemetry opt-out beyond standard VS Code settings.
- Hosted Agent: full — Devin cloud agent integration provides managed agent platform.
- Custom Model Provider: partial — BYOK for Anthropic models (Claude Sonnet 4, Opus 4) for Free/Pro users. No generic custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint configuration documented.
- Pricing: Free $0, Pro $20/mo, Max $200/mo, Teams $40/user/mo
- Notes: Windsurf by Codeium (acquired by Cognition AI in April 2026) is a standalone VS Code fork IDE with integrated coding agent (Cascade). Enterprise analytics API only; no agent embed SDK. Competes directly with Cursor IDE.
